# THE DEEP SALTS — Character Sheet (Live State)
*DM-maintained. Rewritten after any significant change. Single source of truth for HP, tracks, charges, limbs, Insight, and inventory mid-fight.*

---

## "Prettyboy" Lloyd

**Concept:** Young, athletic fighter. Blonde, blue-eyed, weary. Self-made twin knuckledusters rigged with detonating powder capsules.

**Level:** 2 · **Insight:** 6 *(Tier 3 — up from 4: first sighting of a new archetype + reading substantive lore, session 2)*

### Attributes
| VIG | END | STR | SKI | RES |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 8 | 10 | 4 | 17 | 3 |

### Derived Stats
- **Max HP:** 108 (current: 108 — full restoration at hub)
- **Movement budget:** 8m *(base raised 5→8, session 2 movement rework — formula now 8 + ⌊End÷15⌋)*
- **Attribute Modifier (generic uncertain-action checks only, session 2):** Skill 17 → **+0** · Strength 4 → **−2** · Resolve 3 → **−2** · Insight not used for this table
- **Insanity/Influence save mod:** ⌊Resolve÷10⌋ = +0 (Resolve 3 — still below the 10 threshold, no change yet)
- *(Visceral charges removed as a resource — house ruling, session zero: parries gated by timing + real opening only, not a charge pool)*
- *(Standard enemies cannot parry/visceral Lloyd as of session 2 — that loop is boss/elite-tier only)*

### Weapon — Metal Knuckles (paired)
- Type: Quick · Stat/Grade: Skill / D · One-handed, paired
- Base Damage: 22
- Light = 22 + ESV×0.5 · Heavy = 44 + ESV×0.75
- **Current (ESV 17):** Light 30 (costs 1m) · Heavy 57 (costs 3m, session 2 movement rework)
- **Passive Gimmick — Powder Charge:** Load a capsule into a knuckle (Fast Action, 0m). Landing a Heavy with one loaded triggers a detonation stopwatch check:
  - **Hit window:** +50% of that heavy's raw damage; target takes 1 Discombobulation (Tick).
  - **Miss window:** capsule vents wrong; Lloyd takes 1 self-inflicted Blood Loss stack.
  - Capsule consumed either way.
- **Capsules carried:** 4/4 · restocked at hub (spent 3, all successful hits, session 2)

### Status Tracks
| Track | Value |
|---|---|
| Blood Loss | 0 |
| Insanity | 0 |
| Influence | 0 |
| Discombobulation | — (expired) |
| Corrosion | 0 |
| Burning | — |
| Fugue | N/A |

### Limbs
Standard, no mutations, no damage.

### White Salts
- Purse: 0
- Vault: 15 *(9 + 6 banked from session 2 run)*

### Inventory
- Metal Knuckles ×2 (equipped)
- Powder capsules ×4/4
- Wrap ×1 (Quick Item — clears 2 Blood Loss)
- Charm ×1 (Action Item — Insight-gated, needs Insight 6+ — **now usable, Insight sits exactly at 6**)
- **The Beckoner** (idol, Action Item, 2m) — point at one creature in line of sight, speak an invitation: target takes 1 Influence stack (mirrors Lloyd's own Influence rules onto them). Cost: each use puts 1 Influence stack on Lloyd, sourced from the idol itself — doesn't clear via the standard source-elimination rule while he's carrying it; only clears by dropping/Vaulting it.
- Wooden Token (keepsake, no mechanical effect — belonged to "R.")
- Source-water flask (clean variant) — big heal, no Influence stack (gift, unlike the standard flask)
- (starter gear pending — hub intake)

### Known Recipes
- The Choir Deep — identified via Maud, unexplored, still unbrewed, left in his room

### Mutations
None.

---
*Last updated: end of session 2 — full run banked (Purse 6 → Vault), Insight 4→6 (Charm now usable), capsules restocked to 4/4, HP restored to full. Idol identified as "The Beckoner" via NPC lore, not Maud/Insight. Gained a recurring NPC contact (the old man, location unconfirmed persistence across rerolls) and an open request from him (bring clean source-water). New gear: Beckoner idol, Wooden Token, clean Source-water flask.*

content.text:
1	# THE DEEP SALTS — Campaign Log
     2	*Narrative continuity only. For mechanical state see `deep-salts-character-sheet.md`. For rules/design history see the ruleset, design bible, and dev log. If you're a fresh instance picking this up cold, read this file first, then the character sheet.*
     3	
     4	---
     5	
     6	## Where things stand
     7	
     8	**Prettyboy Lloyd** just completed his first run and is currently at the hub (Stillwell Hydro), having banked his Vault, leveled up with the Pool Warden, and identified a new recipe ("the Choir Deep") with Maud. He has not yet descended again. Session zero ended here — next session should pick up either at the hub (shopping, prepping, talking to NPCs) or with him choosing to brew the Choir Deep recipe and descend into a new biome.
     9	
    10	## Open narrative threads (don't forget these)
    11	
    12	- **The old man at the lantern.** Found kneeling in the first biome's flooded colonnade, partially mutated (wrong hand, too many joints), stuck behind an Insight-gated door he can no longer pass. Lloyd tried carrying him through the threshold physically — per the session's ruling, the door only judges the person crossing, not what they carry, so the old man simply didn't come through. No confirmation either way whether this helped, hurt, or did nothing. He's presumably still exactly where Lloyd left him unless a future session revisits that location.
    13	- **The reception clerk's "brother" comments.** The clerk has twice referenced Lloyd's "brother" currently taking the cure, and later said "your brother asked about ways too, near the end" — deliberately unconfirmed whether this is a real person, a mistake, manipulation, or something the location does to everyone who walks in. Lloyd has flatly denied having a brother both times. Unresolved on purpose — a genuine hook, not a loose end to tidy up.
    14	- **The Choir Deep.** Recipe identified, unbrewed. Next biome whenever Lloyd chooses to walk in.
    15	- **The Charm.** Carried, currently unusable — needs Insight Tier 3 (Insight 6+); Lloyd's at Insight 4 (Tier 2). Will become active once he levels Insight further.
    16	
    17	## Established tone / running notes for continuity
    18	
    19	- Combat resolves fast and concrete — numbers first, minimal narrative padding on routine hits. Description gets more room for kills, called shots, and narrative beats (NPC dialogue, environment, discoveries).
    20	- Player prefers real stopwatch checks (their own phone/count) over blind in-chat timing widgets where possible — the blind widget attempt in session zero was a genuine miss on DM's part, not a mechanic to repeat.
    21	- Session-zero rulings (visceral charges removed, Influence uncapped, enemy limb stagger duration, etc.) are now written directly into ruleset v7.1 and design bible v2.6 — a fresh instance reading those documents will already have them; no need to re-explain or re-litigate.
    22	- Jake (the player) is also this game's designer — expect him to spot rule inconsistencies mid-play and to want them resolved with concrete numbers on the spot, not deferred. Flag gaps rather than silently patching them, same as the design-process norms already established.
    23	
    24	---
    25	*Last updated: end of session zero.*
    26

{"language": "markdown", "code": "# THE DEEP SALTS — Campaign Log
*Narrative continuity only. For mechanical state see `deep-salts-character-sheet.md`. For rules/design history see the ruleset, design bible, and dev log. If you're a fresh instance picking this up cold, read this file first, then the character sheet.*

---

## Where things stand

**Prettyboy Lloyd** just completed his first run and is currently at the hub (Stillwell Hydro), having banked his Vault, leveled up with the Pool Warden, and identified a new recipe ("the Choir Deep") with Maud. He has not yet descended again. Session zero ended here — next session should pick up either at the hub (shopping, prepping, talking to NPCs) or with him choosing to brew the Choir Deep recipe and descend into a new biome.

## Open narrative threads (don't forget these)

- **The old man at the lantern.** Found kneeling in the first biome's flooded colonnade, partially mutated (wrong hand, too many joints), stuck behind an Insight-gated door he can no longer pass. Lloyd tried carrying him through the threshold physically — per the session's ruling, the door only judges the person crossing, not what they carry, so the old man simply didn't come through. No confirmation either way whether this helped, hurt, or did nothing. He's presumably still exactly where Lloyd left him unless a future session revisits that location.
- **The reception clerk's "brother" comments.** The clerk has twice referenced Lloyd's "brother" currently taking the cure, and later said "your brother asked about ways too, near the end" — deliberately unconfirmed whether this is a real person, a mistake, manipulation, or something the location does to everyone who walks in. Lloyd has flatly denied having a brother both times. Unresolved on purpose — a genuine hook, not a loose end to tidy up.
- **The Choir Deep.** Recipe identified, unbrewed. Next biome whenever Lloyd chooses to walk in.
- **The Charm.** Carried, currently unusable — needs Insight Tier 3 (Insight 6+); Lloyd's at Insight 4 (Tier 2). Will become active once he levels Insight further.

## Established tone / running notes for continuity

- Combat resolves fast and concrete — numbers first, minimal narrative padding on routine hits. Description gets more room for kills, called shots, and narrative beats (NPC dialogue, environment, discoveries).
- Player prefers real stopwatch checks (their own phone/count) over blind in-chat timing widgets where possible — the blind widget attempt in session zero was a genuine miss on DM's part, not a mechanic to repeat.
- Session-zero rulings (visceral charges removed, Influence uncapped, enemy limb stagger duration, etc.) are now written directly into ruleset v7.1 and design bible v2.6 — a fresh instance reading those documents will already have them; no need to re-explain or re-litigate.
- Jake (the player) is also this game's designer — expect him to spot rule inconsistencies mid-play and to want them resolved with concrete numbers on the spot, not deferred. Flag gaps rather than silently patching them, same as the design-process norms already established.

---
*Last updated: end of session zero.*

input.description: Update "Where things stand" for end of session 2
input.new_str:
**Prettyboy Lloyd** just completed his second real run and is back at the hub (Stillwell Hydro), Vault banked, HP and capsules restored, Insight risen to 6 (Tier 3 — the Charm is now usable for the first time). He entered the same salt recipe as his first run (rerolled layout), fought past a rooted, leashed floor-creature in a flooded gallery without killing it, looted a chapel room (a sentient-seeming idol later identified as "The Beckoner," a keepsake token, a burned journal), met a recurring old man NPC who appears to persist across rerolls of this recipe specifically, and found this run's anchor before backtracking to explore further and banking clean. The Choir Deep recipe is still unbrewed, still sitting in his room. Next session should pick up at the hub — shopping/prepping, trying the now-usable Charm, deciding on the Beckoner's Influence-cost tradeoff, or finally brewing the Choir Deep to descend somewhere new.

## Open narrative threads (don't forget these)

- **The old man at the lantern / by the fire.** First met partially mutated behind an Insight-gated door in the flooded colonnade (session zero/one). Met again this session, in a completely different layout of the *same recipe*, past a rubble crawl, living by a fire, seemingly unaffected by rerolls — "whatever I am now, I'm not one of the things that gets reshuffled with the room." Claims to have run this game's version of salt-recipe runs "more times than I can count," until one run he touched the clean thing and "didn't end up back at any Hydro." No names allowed in conversation with him — his rule, unexplained. He asked Lloyd, unprompted, for a taste of genuinely clean source-water if he finds one. Confirmed talkative, not hostile, currently the best lead on how this world actually works.
- **"R."** — a recurring initial: the burned journal's author, and the name scratched into the wall of a cell with signs of long confinement ("R. WAS HERE"), where Lloyd found a wooden keepsake token. Journal implies R. stopped existing partway through writing it, possibly connected to the Beckoner idol's warning ("if it asks for your name, don't—"). Not yet connected to the old man or anyone else. Fully open.
- **The reception clerk's "brother" comments.** Now referenced three times total (twice in session zero/one, confirmed still live this session — Lloyd told the old man "she's mentioned a brother twice now"). Lloyd has not confirmed or denied having a brother to the old man, deliberately avoiding a direct answer both to the clerk previously and now. Still an open, deliberate hook.
- **The dragging floor-creature.** Scouted, fought (twice grabbed Lloyd, twice broken via Heavy + Powder Charge), never killed — Lloyd disengaged through a doorway once clear of its reach. Per the old man, it's rooted, non-aggressive unless provoked, has been in the flood gallery for years chasing something that "isn't native" and "never found its way home," and per the old man's own words is now audibly sulking after being "just punched" rather than tricked or bribed like everything before it. HP currently sits at 468/original pool if the same instance persists into a future reroll of this recipe — unconfirmed whether it does.
- **The Beckoner (idol).** Identified via the old man, not Maud or an Insight roll. Forces an Influence stack onto a target in line of sight; costs Lloyd 1 Influence stack per use, sourced from the idol itself, which does NOT clear via the normal source-elimination rule while he's still carrying it. A real risk/reward item — unused so far.
- **The Choir Deep.** Recipe identified, still unbrewed, still in his room. Two full sessions now without touching it.
- **The Charm.** Was unusable at Insight 4; now sits exactly at the Insight 6 threshold and is live for the first time. Untested.
- **The old man's request.** Wants a taste of genuinely clean source-water, if Lloyd finds a source that qualifies. Low-stakes, easy to forget, explicitly optional per the old man himself.
input.old_str:
**Prettyboy Lloyd** just completed his first run and is currently at the hub (Stillwell Hydro), having banked his Vault, leveled up with the Pool Warden, and identified a new recipe ("the Choir Deep") with Maud. He has not yet descended again. Session zero ended here — next session should pick up either at the hub (shopping, prepping, talking to NPCs) or with him choosing to brew the Choir Deep recipe and descend into a new biome.

## Open narrative threads (don't forget these)

- **The old man at the lantern.** Found kneeling in the first biome's flooded colonnade, partially mutated (wrong hand, too many joints), stuck behind an Insight-gated door he can no longer pass. Lloyd tried carrying him through the threshold physically — per the session's ruling, the door only judges the person crossing, not what they carry, so the old man simply didn't come through. No confirmation either way whether this helped, hurt, or did nothing. He's presumably still exactly where Lloyd left him unless a future session revisits that location.
- **The reception clerk's "brother" comments.** The clerk has twice referenced Lloyd's "brother" currently taking the cure, and later said "your brother asked about ways too, near the end" — deliberately unconfirmed whether this is a real person, a mistake, manipulation, or something the location does to everyone who walks in. Lloyd has flatly denied having a brother both times. Unresolved on purpose — a genuine hook, not a loose end to tidy up.
- **The Choir Deep.** Recipe identified, unbrewed. Next biome whenever Lloyd chooses to walk in.
- **The Charm.** Carried, currently unusable — needs Insight Tier 3 (Insight 6+); Lloyd's at Insight 4 (Tier 2). Will become active once he levels Insight further.

content.text:
Both pushed. Quick summary of where session 2 leaves things:

- **HP:** 108/108 · **Capsules:** 4/4 · **Insight:** 6 (Charm now live) · **Vault:** 15
- **New gear:** The Beckoner (idol), Wooden Token, clean Source-water flask
- **Big rules changes locked in:** scaled Attribute Modifier table, movement-as-action-fuel rework (8m base, actions cost movement), enemy parries restricted to boss-tier, Effigy reclassified as a boss
- **Open threads:** the old man's persistence and his water request, "R.", the brother comments, the dragger (alive, 468 HP if it carries over), the Beckoner's Influence cost, and the still-unbrewed Choir Deep

That's session 2 in the can whenever you're ready to close out — or if you want to keep going tonight, just say the word.

The dragger, "R." and the Beckoner idol seem to me like instance-based storylines - once Lloyd leaves that instance, they are lost. This is because enemies and environmental stories would not carry over into new iterations. Someone named "R" died in that instance, but we will go to a whole new instance next time so we will probably not see any evidence of them. Similarly, we might encounter another Dragger, but it will be a new instance of a dragger, not the same one. The old man seems to be unique in that he seems to be in multiple, or maybe all, instances. The Beckoner idol is arguably the only one of those three "instance quests" that we completed - we encountered it, recovered it and figured out what it does, getting a reward for doing so.

So lets make a note in the documentation that "Instance Quests" are only active until the player leaves that instance - after that, whatever is not done is lost to the void / shifting reality / whatever. We don't need to track those quests. We DO need to track "Quests" - the ones that carry over into new instances, biomes, or into the Hydro. This is where the old man, the comments about the brother, and the Choir Deep quests go.

Make sense? You can probably word it to yourself more betterer and keep it more LLM friendly for context windows and stuff.

content.text:
Makes complete sense — it also gives future-me a clean triage rule: if a thread lives in the layout, it dies with the layout. Only things anchored to persistent entities (NPCs like the old man, the hub, recipes, carried items) get tracked. The Beckoner is a good example of the lifecycle working: instance quest encountered, completed in-instance, reward extracted as a *persistent item* — which is the only way instance content escapes the reroll.

One nuance I'll encode: the dragger's 468 HP note is now officially garbage — new instance, new dragger, full pool. That was already sitting in the campaign log as a maybe; this ruling deletes the maybe.

content.name: str_replace

input.description: Add Instance Quests vs persistent Quests to ruleset §18
input.new_str:
## 18. Salts Rooms & Biomes — Exploration Structure

**Instance Quests vs Quests (Session 2 ruling).** Narrative content in a run splits cleanly in two:

- **Instance Quests** — threads that live inside one layout: environmental stories, corpses, journals, one-off creatures, unexplored doors. They expire the moment the player exits (anchor, retreat, or death → reroll). Unfinished instance content is lost to the shifting reality — permanently, no tracking, no guilt. Completing one during the run can convert its reward into something persistent (an item carried out, lore learned, Insight gained) — that's the only way instance content escapes the reroll.
- **Quests** — threads anchored to persistent entities: recurring NPCs, the hub and its people, known recipes, carried items. These survive rerolls and are the only narrative threads worth tracking between sessions.
- **Corollary:** enemies do not persist across instances. A new run may contain the same *kind* of creature, but it's a fresh instance at full pool — no carrying over damage dealt in a previous layout.

**DM triage rule:** if a thread is anchored to the layout, it dies with the layout. Only log what's anchored to something persistent.

input.new_str:
## Quests (persistent — track these)

*Per the Instance Quest ruling (Ruleset §18, session 2): only threads anchored to persistent entities — recurring NPCs, the hub, recipes, carried items — survive rerolls. Everything else died with its layout and is deliberately not tracked.*

- **The old man by the fire.** The one known reroll-persistent NPC. First met partially mutated behind an Insight-gated door (session zero/one); met again this session in a completely different layout of the same recipe — "whatever I am now, I'm not one of the things that gets reshuffled with the room." Claims to have run this world's version of recipe-runs countless times until one run he touched the clean thing and "didn't end up back at any Hydro." No-names rule when speaking with him, unexplained. Talkative, trades honest question for honest question, currently the best lead on how the world works. **Open request:** bring him a taste of genuinely clean source-water if one is found (explicitly optional, his words). **Open question:** whether he persists in *other* recipes too — he doesn't know himself; brewing the Choir Deep will test it.
- **The reception clerk's "brother" comments.** Referenced multiple times across sessions; Lloyd has avoided confirming or denying having a brother. Clerk is the hub's designated recurring-antagonist figure. Deliberate long-burn hook, still open.
- **The Choir Deep.** Recipe identified, unbrewed, sitting in Lloyd's room two sessions running. Doubles as the persistence experiment for the old man.
- **The Beckoner (carried item).** Instance quest *completed* — encountered, recovered, identified in-run, extracted as a persistent item. Its ongoing tradeoff (1 Influence stack on Lloyd per use, uncleared while carried) is live whenever he first uses it. Its warning lore ("if it asks for your name, don't—") travels with it.
- **The Charm (carried item).** Now usable for the first time — Insight sits exactly at its 6+ gate. Untested.
- **The Wooden Token (carried item).** Keepsake of "R.," a person from a now-expired instance. No mechanical effect; kept for whatever narrative weight it accrues. The R. story itself is gone with its layout — the token is all that escaped.

## Expired instance quests (session 2 — not tracked, listed once for flavor continuity only)

The dragger (fought, never killed — any future dragger is a fresh instance at full pool), "R." and the cell, the burned journal's author, the chapel and its offerings, the scratched door, both unexplored forks. All lost to the reroll, as intended.

content.text:
Pushed — ruleset §18, campaign log, and dev log all updated. The campaign log now splits into a tracked **Quests** section (old man, brother, Choir Deep, Beckoner/Charm/Token as carried items) and a one-time flavor list of what expired with the layout. Dragger's HP note is deleted; any future dragger starts fresh.

One design observation for free: this ruling quietly makes the old man *more* interesting — he's now definitionally an anomaly, the one thing that violates the instance rule. Whatever explains him has to explain that.

That's session 2 fully closed out unless there's anything else.
i think we're fully done. what's notable on our character sheet right now? Gimme a summary of how Lloyd is doing

Lloyd's in the best shape he's been since character creation, honestly:

**The headline:** Insight 6. That's the big one — two thresholds crossed at once. The Charm is live for the first time, and 6 was the gate on that Insight-locked door from the first run, so doors of that tier are now openable. The Faustian catch: Insight also raises his Insanity/Influence susceptibility floor, and he just picked up an item whose whole gimmick is feeding him Influence stacks.

**Resources:** 108/108 HP, 4/4 capsules, 15 Vault Salts, empty Purse, zero status tracks. Completely clean slate. 15 Salts is real leveling money — worth a Pool Warden visit before the next descent.

**The Beckoner** is the most interesting thing he owns and the most dangerous. Influence-mirror on demand, but every use stacks Influence on *him* with no clear while carried. With Resolve 3 giving him a +0 save mod, he's badly built to resist his own item. It's a trap for exactly his stat spread — which is presumably the point.

**The soft spots haven't moved:** Strength 4 and Resolve 3 are both −2 on the new modifier table, and Vigor 8 means his HP pool is basically starter-tier. He's a glass skirmisher who went three-for-three on stopwatch checks — the build works, but only while the timing holds.

**Loadout oddity:** one Wrap, two heals (Tonic's not even listed — worth checking hub intake), and "starter gear pending" has now survived two full sessions on the sheet. Might be time to actually resolve that line.

Solid session. Lloyd punched a landmark, made a friend, and got richer — by Deep Salts standards that's a flawless run.
lets suss out that loadout oddity then.

content.text:
{"returncode":0,"stdout":"185-
186-### Damage Floor & Scale
187-
188:Numbers run high, not d20-fantasy-modest — the ESV and multiplier maths only stays clean at scale. **20 is the practical floor for any base number that passes through a multiplier** (weapon bases, enemy attack damage, DM-generated numbers). 20 keeps results whole across the multipliers that actually come up (×0.65 = 13, ×0.75 = 15, ×1.5 = 30), and anything lower makes weak weapons unable to sever, ever — not the intent (a kitchen knife is still a knife). The floor doesn't bind flat, unmodified numbers (a Quick Item heal, a status tick).
189-
190-Enemy HP (§16) and thresholds (§10) scale to match: archetype HP band × level multiplier (§17), attack damage proportionate to what Max HP and Rally (§6) can absorb — a mook stings, a boss's telegraphed haymaker is a real chunk that Rally can partially forgive.
191-
192----
193-
194-## 5. Status Tracks
195-
196-Most conditions are tracks — counters that build (0–10) rather than switches. Defaults below; tune to taste.
197-
198:**BLOOD LOSS** — 1 stack per qualifying slashing/piercing hit or hazard. Each new stack deals immediate bonus damage equal to the new stack total (0→1: +1; 1→2: +2; five consecutive cuts = 15 bonus total). No passive drain between hits. Cap 10 → triggers Rupture, resets to 0. Clear: bandage, cautery, or a pressure action (a Wrap clears 2 stacks, §8).
199-
200-**RUPTURE** (triggered only, at Blood Loss 10) — 25% of Max HP direct damage, staggers you a beat, resets Blood Loss to 0. Low Rally recovery — ten unmanaged stacks are meant to stick. **(untested)**
201-
202-**INSANITY** — the "character acts without you" meter. On taking damage, witnessing something wrong, or failing a fear save: Save Roll (below). Fail → DM controls you one turn (freeze, flee, strike the nearest body, speak to something that isn't there). High Insight raises this. *Not the same as Insight.*
203-
204-**BURNING** — ticking damage each turn. Spreads to oil, cloth, steam-soaked surfaces. Action to smother. Heat sources can re-ignite you.
205-
206-**DISCOMBOBULATION** — a Tick, not a track. Applied at 1–3 (DM's call by severity: scrambled sign = 1, the source-spring stair = 3), counts down 1 at end of each of your turns automatically. While active the DM may misdescribe exits, reverse movement, strip reactions, or scramble attack directions — pick what fits. A fresh application refreshes to the new value if higher. Cap 3. (Long-term cousin: Fugue, §13 — a Track, not a Tick.)
207-
208-**INFLUENCE** — some enemies suggest rather than strike, verbally or otherwise — a spoken command, or just a pull, an ache, a wordless want. Builds as a Track like Blood Loss, but **uncapped past 10** — no fixed overflow trigger, no Rupture-equivalent. Instead, **the severity of compelled effects available to the DM scales with stack count**: low stacks might mean lingering near a source or a minor compulsion; higher stacks can escalate to dropping a weapon, fleeing, self-harm, or striking an ally. This is deliberately open-ended DM discretion, not a fixed table — calibrate severity to stack count and use judgment so it doesn't wreck pacing. On a failed save, obey the effect (or the enemy's spoken command, if it has one) on your next turn. Susceptibility rises with Insanity and Insight.
--
279-Costs above are the defaults — specific weapons and actions may price differently, and perks may refund movement after an action, grant bonus movement before one, or zero a cost under conditions. (Perk design space, not yet populated.)
280-
281-**Fast Action** (one per turn, always 0m):

Mechanically: Retreat (§12) — forfeit the Purse, keep character/limbs, instant, free, usable between any two actions including mid-resolution of an attack. The only counter — a gag/silence effect or an enemy that severed your voice — is rare, boss-tier, always telegraphed.

## 4. Character Creation & Progression

### Backstory-Negotiated Creation

Before numbers, the player writes a short backstory and concept. The DM negotiates the mechanical translation:

- **Attribute reallocation.** Default spread is flat (8 in all five). A strong concept can redistribute the same 40-point pool unevenly. Total stays at 40 unless DM and player agree the concept earns more — always a two-way trade, never a freebie.
- **A bespoke starting weapon.** Instead of the Weapon table: type, governing stat, base damage placed in the existing range for the character's fictional strength (never under the Damage Floor), and one signature gimmick. A gimmick costs something (movement, setup, a self-applied track) for something (bonus damage, a status, positioning) — and any risk-for-reward uses the existing tools (stopwatch or d20 check), not a new resolution system.
- **A negotiation, not a wishlist.** The DM pushes back on anything that breaks the Damage Floor, ignores ESV, or gives something for nothing.

*Worked example on file: a young, pregnant guest with a kitchen knife. Result — low Vigor, knife at the floor, signature two-swipe-then-plunge: plunge deals double swipe damage, costs extra movement to set up, stopwatch check for bonus damage on the plunge.*

### Attributes

Five buyable attributes; Insight is a separate sixth dial (§11), earned not bought.

- **VIGOR** → max HP, Blood Loss resilience
- **ENDURANCE** → movement budget, visceral charges
- **STRENGTH** → heavy weapon damage, limb-sever power
- **SKILL** → light weapon damage, parry/precision tolerance
- **RESOLVE** → Insanity/Influence saves

All start at 8. Cap 99. Starting Insight = 1.

**Derived stats** (linear, from raw score):

- Max HP = 60 + (Vigor × 6) → Vigor 8 = 108, Vigor 99 = 654
- Movement budget = 8 + ⌊Endurance ÷ 15⌋ metres → End 8 = 8m, End 99 = 14m
- Insanity/Influence save bonus = ⌊Resolve ÷ 10⌋ → Resolve 8 = +0, Resolve 99 = +9

*(Visceral charges removed as a derived stat — session zero ruling. Parries are now gated entirely by timing accuracy and a genuine telegraphed opening, not a resource pool; see §9.)*

### Effective Scaling Value (ESV) — soft caps

Each point's contribution shrinks by band. Weapon/combat scaling uses ESV; derived stats above use raw score.

186-### Damage Floor & Scale
187-
188:Numbers run high, not d20-fantasy-modest — the ESV and multiplier maths only stays clean at scale. **20 is the practical floor for any base number that passes through a multiplier** (weapon bases, enemy attack damage, DM-generated numbers). 20 keeps results whole across the multipliers that actually come up (×0.65 = 13, ×0.75 = 15, ×1.5 = 30), and anything lower makes weak weapons unable to sever, ever — not the intent (a kitchen knife is still a knife). The floor doesn't bind flat, unmodified numbers (a Quick Item heal, a status tick).
189-
190-Enemy HP (§16) and thresholds (§10) scale to match: archetype HP band × level multiplier (§17), attack damage proportionate to what Max HP and Rally (§6) can absorb — a mook stings, a boss's telegraphed haymaker is a real chunk that Rally can partially forgive.

195-
196-Most conditions are tracks — counters that build (0–10) rather than switches. Defaults below; tune to taste.
197-
198:**BLOOD LOSS** — 1 stack per qualifying slashing/piercing hit or hazard. Each new stack deals immediate bonus damage equal to the new stack total (0→1: +1; 1→2: +2; five consecutive cuts = 15 bonus total). No passive drain between hits. Cap 10 → triggers Rupture, resets to 0. Clear: bandage, cautery, or a pressure action (a Wrap clears 2 stacks, §8).
199-
200-**RUPTURE** (triggered only, at Blood Loss 10) — 25% of Max HP direct damage, staggers you a beat, resets Blood Loss to 0. Low Rally recovery — ten unmanaged stacks are meant to stick. **(untested)**
201-
202-**INSANITY** — the "character acts without you" meter. On taking damage, witnessing something wrong, or failing a fear save: Save Roll (below). Fail → DM controls you one turn (freeze, flee, strike the nearest body, speak to something that isn't there). High Insight raises this. *Not the same as Insight.*
203-
204-**BURNING** — ticking damage each turn. Spreads to oil, cloth, steam-soaked surfaces. Action to smother. Heat sources can re-ignite you.
205-
206-**DISCOMBOBULATION** — a Tick, not a track. Applied at 1–3 (DM's call by severity: scrambled sign = 1, the source-spring stair = 3), counts down 1 at end of each of your turns automatically. 208-**INFLUENCE** — some enemies suggest rather than strike, verbally or otherwise — a spoken command, or just a pull, an ache, a wordless want. Builds as a Track like Blood Loss, but **uncapped past 10** — no fixed overflow trigger, no Rupture-equivalent. This is deliberately open-ended DM discretion, not a fixed table — calibrate severity to stack count and use judgment so it doesn't wreck pacing. content.text:
Here's the actual state of it, traced: the rules define starting *weapon* negotiation thoroughly and starting *items* not at all. There's no rule anywhere granting a starting kit — the Wrap and Charm on Lloyd's sheet were session-zero ad-hoc grants, and "(starter gear pending — hub intake)" was a placeholder I (or a previous instance) wrote as an IOU that no rule ever backed. It's a genuine gap, not an unresolved fetch quest: **character creation grants a weapon but no consumables, and nothing defines what a hub restock actually stocks.**

Also connected: the capsule restock. "Restocked at hub" works for Lloyd's signature gimmick, but Wraps/Tonics/Salts/Scours have no acquisition rule at all — no shop, no price, no free allotment.

Proposal, keeping it one clean rule instead of a whole economy:

**Hub Kit (§14 addition):** On every hub arrival, the character's consumable kit refills to its standard loadout, free — same logic as HP and signature-weapon ammo. Default kit: **2× Wrap, 2× Tonic, 1× Salts, 1× Scour.** Unused items don't stack past the kit cap. Anything *beyond* the kit (extra flasks, thrown vials, cautery iron) is found in runs or bought from hub vendors with White Salts — that's the actual item economy, sitting on top of a guaranteed floor.

Rationale: it keeps runs from being lost to bad shopping, makes the kit a known quantity for encounter design, and gives White Salts something to buy besides levels without me inventing a price list tonight.

Lloyd's sheet under this: Wrap ×2, Tonic ×2, Salts ×1, Scour ×1, "starter gear pending" line deleted, clean Source-water flask sits outside the kit as found-gear.

Sign off, tweak the kit contents, or different approach entirely?
sounds good. before you add them all to the docs though, what does each item do?

Straight from the ruleset as written:

**Kit items (all Quick Items — Fast Action, 0m, usable alongside an attack):**
- **Wrap** — clears 2 Blood Loss stacks
- **Tonic** — small flat heal, ~10% max HP (11 HP for Lloyd)
- **Salts** — clears 1 Insanity tick
- **Scour** — clears 1 Corrosion stack without costing your turn

**Non-kit items already defined (Action Items — full Action, 2m, strong but cost the turn):**
- **Source-water flask** — big heal, but adds an Influence stack (Lloyd's clean variant skips the stack — gift, one-off)
- **Cautery iron** — fully clears Blood Loss, but costs your full remaining movement
- **Thrown vial** — applies Corrosion or Burning at range
- **Charm** — one-shot buff (extra die, widened parry window), Insight-gated

One honest flag: "Salts clears 1 Insanity tick" is slightly off-model — Insanity is written as a save-triggering track, not a tick like Discombobulation, and the ruleset never defines what a "tick" of it means. Also awkward that a consumable named Salts shares a name with the currency. Neither breaks tonight's change; both worth a cleanup pass eventually. Want me to note the Insanity-wording gap in the dev log while I'm in there, or leave it?

Otherwise, confirming the kit as 2× Wrap, 2× Tonic, 1× Salts, 1× Scour and pushing.
let's change the insanity-clearing item now. Insanity builds over time and doesn't tick down, right? So maybe the item that clears it is something that grounds or calms a character

content.text:
Pushed. Loadout oddity fully resolved:

- **Hub Kit** (§14): 2× Wrap, 2× Tonic, 1× Camphor, 1× Scour — free refill every hub arrival, capped, non-stacking. Everything beyond it is found or bought.
- **Camphor** replaces the Salts item — clears 2 Insanity stacks, name-clash dead, effect actually defined now.
- Character sheet updated: kit listed, "starter gear pending" placeholder finally deleted after two sessions of squatting.

Lloyd now heads into session 3 with a full kit, full capsules, 15 Vault Salts to spend, a live Charm, and a cursed idol he hasn't dared use yet. Solid position.

That's everything sussed unless something else is nagging at you.

oh, and the bottle that the old man gave us - is that renewable or not? seems powerful if it is, but still valuable as a one-off if not. i could see it going either way. Same with the Charm - does it also carry over?

Good instinct to check both, and they're not the same case:

**The flask: one-off, and the docs already say so — but with a boundary worth stating explicitly.** The rules-as-written case is clean: the Hub Kit rule lists Source-water flasks as explicitly *outside* the kit ("acquired in runs or bought"), and the sheet already tags it "gift, outside the kit — doesn't refill." Design-wise it can't be renewable anyway: a repeatable big heal with no Influence tax invalidates the standard flask's entire power-for-vulnerability tradeoff, which is the item's whole identity. And narratively the old man handed over a specific jar of a specific batch — "no tax attached to *this* batch" was doing load-bearing work in that sentence.

The one genuine ambiguity: it's an *item*, and hub restoration restores a lot of things. So stating the boundary plainly — **hub refills the Kit and signature ammo; found/gifted/bought consumables never refill, gone when used.** The clean flask is a one-shot. Spend it on a moment that deserves it.

**The Charm: carries over, but it's a different question entirely.** It's not a consumable in the kit sense — it's a carried Insight-gated item, same persistence category as the Beckoner and the Token. It survives hub trips fine. What the docs *don't* answer: it's written as a "one-shot buff," and nothing says whether a spent Charm is destroyed forever or recharges somehow. That's a genuine undefined, not me hedging. My lean: **consumed on use, gone forever** — one-shots should cost something real, and "found a Charm" stays a meaningful loot moment. But it's your call.

Want me to lock in "Charm: destroyed on use" plus the found-consumables-never-refill line, and push both?

I think destroyed on use is fair, yes. I think that moving forward you should include a line for items that notes whether it is: 
"constant" (can be carried over, can be used as many times as the player wants - the Idol), 
"added to Hub Kit" (recharges and resets on return to Hydro - the knuckle capsules), 
"Uses: [number]" (has this many uses, then no longer works. Might be rechargable in other ways but not from returning to the hydro - The Charm) 
"Instance-specific" (cannot be taken out of the instance it is found within - the journal we found in the last instance would be one of these if I'd kept it, as it was only useful in that one instance)

summaries.summary: Thinking about categorizing game items by persistence and recharge mechanics.
Categorizing items within durability taxonomy framework.
Architected durability taxonomy and catalogued items systematically.

content.text: That's a clean taxonomy — four tags covering every acquisition and persistence question in one line per item. Writing it in.

input.description: Add item durability tag system to §8 Items
input.new_str:
### Items

**Durability tags (Session 2 ruling).** Every item carries exactly one tag answering "what happens to this over time":

- **Constant** — persists indefinitely, unlimited uses. Equipment, keepsakes, the Beckoner. Any per-use *cost* (like the Beckoner's Influence stack) still applies; the tag only means the item itself never runs out.
- **Hub Kit** — recharges/resets free on every return to the hub. The standard consumable kit (§14) and signature-weapon ammo (e.g. powder capsules).
- **Uses: [N]** — works N times, then it's spent. NOT refilled by the hub; may be rechargeable by other in-world means if one is ever found. The Charm is Uses: 1 — destroyed on use.
- **Instance-specific** — cannot leave the layout it was found in; expires with the instance (see §18). Journals, keys, environmental tools that only mean something where they lie.

When an item enters play, the DM assigns its tag on the spot.
input.old_str: ### Items

content.text:
Pushed. Every item in the game now answers the persistence question in one tag, and both your questions are settled in the docs: **clean flask — Uses: 1** (a renewable no-tax big heal would gut the standard flask's whole tradeoff), **Charm — Uses: 1, destroyed on use** (finding one stays a real loot moment).

Lloyd's sheet is fully tagged, and the tag system means this category of question never needs relitigating — new item enters play, it gets a tag on the spot.

That's session 2 well and truly done, I reckon. Repo's current across all five docs. See you and Lloyd for session 3 — the Choir Deep's been patient long enough.
